title: PBX Extensions
lede: Give every desk, department, or AI agent its own short internal number — like 101 or 202 — on your own SIP trunk. Import an extension in under a minute, point it at an AI agent or forward it anywhere, and dial between extensions like a full office phone system.

steps.title: Open PBX Extensions
steps.content:
A PBX extension is a short internal number (for example 101) that lives on your own SIP provider or office PBX and is imported into iMash. Once imported, it behaves like any other number on the platform: it can be answered by an AI agent, forwarded to a person, and every call it makes or receives is logged.

  1. In the left sidebar, under Call Center, click PBX Extensions. (You can also use the PBX Extensions tile in the Contact Center tab strip at the top.)

The list shows one row per extension with these columns:

  • Extension — the full stored number (for example +0012101). Click it to open the extension's details.
  • Label — a friendly name like "Front Desk"; click the pencil to edit it inline.
  • Inbound Agent — the AI agent that answers calls to this extension.
  • Outgoing / Incoming — running call counters for the extension.
  • Provider — which SIP trunk the extension lives on.
  • Owner — the team member the extension belongs to.
  • Actions — the eye icon opens details; the trash icon deletes the extension.

images.caption: Call Center → PBX Extensions — every internal number in one place.
Call Center → PBX Extensions — every internal number in one place.

steps.title: Before you start: connect a SIP provider
steps.content:
Extensions always live on a SIP trunk that you bring — they are not iMash-purchased numbers. So the one prerequisite is a connected Custom SIP provider.

  1. In the left sidebar, click SIP Providers and make sure your trunk or PBX is listed there.
  2. If it isn't yet, follow the Add a SIP Provider guide first (about 10 minutes), then come back here.
💡 Tip: No separate trip needed — you can also add the provider on the fly from inside the Import Extension window (there's an Add Provider shortcut right in the provider dropdown).

steps.title: Click Import Extension and pick your provider
steps.content:
1. In the top-right of the PBX Extensions page, click Import Extension.
  2. In the SIP Phone Provider dropdown, select the trunk that hosts this extension.
  3. Don't see your trunk? Click + Add Provider at the bottom of the dropdown to create one without leaving the page.

The rest of the form stays disabled until a provider is selected — that's your cue that this field comes first.

images.alt: Import Extension modal with the SIP Phone Provider dropdown open
images.caption: Pick the SIP trunk the extension lives on — or add one right from the dropdown.

images.alt: Import Extension modal with the SIP Phone Provider dropdown open
images.caption: Pick the SIP trunk the extension lives on — or add one right from the dropdown.

steps.title: Fill in the extension details
steps.content:
Three quick fields and you're done:

  1. Label — a friendly name you'll recognize in the list, like "Front Desk" or "Support Line".
  2. Include "+" prefix — leave this checked unless your SIP provider sends extension numbers without a leading +. iMash treats +0012101 and 0012101 as the same extension either way.
  3. Extension Number — type just the short number, 1–10 digits (for example 101). The 0012 prefix shown in front of the field is added automatically — it's how iMash stores extensions so they never collide with real phone numbers. Extension 101 is saved as +0012101.

As you type, iMash checks availability in real time:

  • ✅ "Extension is available!" — you're good to go.
  • ❌ "Extension is taken" — iMash suggests nearby free numbers; click a suggestion tag to use it instantly.
💡 Advanced: If your provider addresses a sub-extension with a #, you can append it — for example 101#999 imports as +0012101#999. Only digits are allowed after the #.

images.alt: Import Extension modal with provider selected
images.caption: Label, prefix choice, and the short extension number — the 0012 prefix is added for you.

images.alt: Import Extension modal with provider selected
images.caption: Label, prefix choice, and the short extension number — the 0012 prefix is added for you.

steps.title: Import — routing is provisioned automatically
steps.content:
1. Click Import Extension at the bottom of the window. (The button stays disabled until the number is valid and a provider is selected.)
  2. iMash saves the extension and sets up the SIP call routing for it in the same step — no manual trunk configuration needed.
  3. When you see "Your Extension has been imported successfully", the new row appears in the list, ready to take calls.
⚠️ Good to know: If SIP routing can't be set up, the extension is not saved — you'll see an "Extension not added" message with the reason, and the window stays open so you can fix the issue (usually the provider's gateway details) and click Import Extension again. You'll never end up with a dead extension that looks live but can't route calls.

steps.title: Choose how the extension answers: AI agent or forward
steps.content:
Now the fun part — deciding what happens when someone dials the extension.

  1. In the list, click the extension number (or the eye icon under Actions). The Extension Details drawer opens on the Settings tab.
  2. Under Inbound Settings, use the Call Handling switch to pick a mode:
    • Enable Agents — an AI agent answers the extension. Pick it in the Inbound Agent dropdown.
    • Enable Forward — calls are passed along instead. Fill in Forward Calls To: with another extension or a full phone number, and optionally pick a Forwarding Agent to handle the call if forwarding is in play.
  3. Click Save Settings (it lights up blue whenever you have unsaved changes).
  4. Want to hear it live? Click Test Call to open the web dialer and talk to the extension's agent right from your browser.

The drawer's two other tabs — Inbound Calls and Outbound Calls — list every call this extension has handled. Click any row to open the full call detail with recording and transcript.

images.alt: Extension Details drawer on the Settings tab in Enable Agents mode
images.caption: Extension Details → Settings — Enable Agents mode: pick the AI agent that answers the extension.

images.alt: Extension Details drawer on the Settings tab in Enable Agents mode
images.caption: Extension Details → Settings — Enable Agents mode: pick the AI agent that answers the extension.

images.alt: Extension Details drawer with Call Handling switched to Enable Forward
images.caption: Switch Call Handling to Enable Forward and the forwarding fields appear — Save Settings lights up blue.

images.alt: Extension Details drawer with Call Handling switched to Enable Forward
images.caption: Switch Call Handling to Enable Forward and the forwarding fields appear — Save Settings lights up blue.

steps.title: Dial between extensions
steps.content:
Extensions turn iMash into your internal phone system:

  • From your SIP phones / PBX: dial the short number (for example 101) and your provider delivers the call to iMash in the 0012 format (+0012101). iMash matches the imported extension and runs its call handling — AI agent or forward.
  • Extension to extension: set one extension's Forward Calls To: field to another extension's number and calls hop straight between them.
  • From a main phone number: any phone number on your platform can forward its calls to an extension — open the number under Phone numbers, switch Call Handling to Enable Forward, and enter the extension. Your main line rings the front desk; the front desk is an extension. Done.
💡 Tip: Because every extension can carry its own AI agent, you can give each department a specialist agent — dial 101 for the booking assistant, 102 for support — all on a single trunk.

steps.title: Manage extensions in bulk: labels, filters, and cleanup
steps.content:
As your extension list grows, the list page keeps it tidy:

  • Filter by label / Filter by owner — the two dropdowns above the table narrow the list instantly; your filters are remembered between visits.
  • Rename inline — click the pencil next to any label, type or pick a new one, and confirm with the check mark.
  • Bulk actions — tick the checkboxes on several rows and three buttons appear: Inbound Agent (assign or clear the answering agent for all selected at once), Edit Label (relabel the whole selection), and Delete Selected.
  • Delete one — click the trash icon on a row and confirm. Deleting an extension stops its routing; you can always re-import the same number later.
  • API Info — the button in the top-right opens ready-made code examples for listing and adding extensions programmatically with your API key.
⚠️ Careful with bulk delete: the confirmation applies to every selected row — double-check the "X selected" counter before you confirm.

images.alt: PBX Extensions list with filters and bulk actions
images.caption: Label and owner filters live above the table; select rows to unlock bulk actions.

images.alt: PBX Extensions list with filters and bulk actions
images.caption: Label and owner filters live above the table; select rows to unlock bulk actions.

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials
